Methods for Bayesian Hidden-Population Models

Source: R/bayes.R
bayes_methods.Rd

S3 methods for Bayesian hidden-population models fitted with estimate_hidden_pop_bayes.

Usage

# S3 method for class 'uncounted_bayes'
summary(
  object,
  total = FALSE,
  level = 0.95,
  diagnostics = TRUE,
  ...
)

# S3 method for class 'uncounted_bayes'
coef(object, summary = c("median", "mean", "draws"), ...)

# S3 method for class 'uncounted_bayes'
fitted(object, summary = c("mean", "median", "draws"), ...)

# S3 method for class 'uncounted_bayes'
predict(
  object,
  newdata = NULL,
  type = c("response", "link", "counts", "draws"),
  summary = TRUE,
  ...
)

# S3 method for class 'uncounted_bayes'
residuals(
  object,
  type = c("response", "pearson"),
  summary = c("mean", "median"),
  ...
)

# S3 method for class 'uncounted_bayes'
loo(object, ...)

# S3 method for class 'uncounted_bayes'
tidy(x, conf.int = FALSE, conf.level = 0.95, ...)

# S3 method for class 'uncounted_bayes'
glance(x, loo = FALSE, ...)

Arguments

object

An "uncounted_bayes" object.

total

Logical; include a total population-size summary in summary().

level

Credible interval level for summaries.

diagnostics

Logical; include MCMC diagnostics in summary().

summary

Posterior summary to return. For coef() and fitted(), use "mean", "median", or "draws". For predict(), use TRUE/"mean", "median", or FALSE to return draws. For residuals(), use "mean" or "median" fitted values.

newdata

Optional data frame for prediction. If NULL, predictions are for the original data.

type

Prediction or residual type. For predict(), "response" returns posterior expected observed counts, "link" returns the log mean, "counts" returns replicated posterior predictive counts, and "draws" returns response-scale expected-count draws. For residuals(), "response" and "pearson" are supported.

x

An "uncounted_bayes" object for broom-style methods.

conf.int

Logical; include credible intervals in tidy().

conf.level

Credible interval level for tidy().

loo

Logical; if TRUE, glance() also computes brms::loo() summaries.

...

Additional arguments passed to the underlying brms posterior prediction or LOO functions where relevant.

Value

summary() invisibly returns a list containing coefficient, population-size, and diagnostic summaries. coef() returns posterior coefficient summaries or draws. fitted() and predict() return numeric summaries or draw matrices. residuals() returns numeric residuals. loo() returns the object from brms::loo(). tidy() and glance() return data frames.

Details

These methods report posterior quantities rather than Wald standard errors and p values. Coefficient tables include posterior means, medians, posterior standard deviations, credible intervals, R-hat, bulk ESS, and tail ESS. Prediction methods delegate to the underlying brms posterior prediction functions.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
summary(fit_bayes, total = TRUE)
coef(fit_bayes)
head(fitted(fit_bayes))
head(predict(fit_bayes, type = "counts", summary = FALSE))
head(residuals(fit_bayes))
tidy(fit_bayes, conf.int = TRUE)
glance(fit_bayes)
loo(fit_bayes)
} # }
